heading: Omitted
label: 1 Omitted
notes: CodificationSection, , , provided that the Division of Insular Affairs of the War Department should be known as the Bureau of Insular Affairs and prescribed its business.act July 1, 1902, ch. 1369, § 8732 Stat. 712By Ex. Ord. No. 6726, eff. , the Division of Territories and Island Possessions was established in the Department of the Interior, and the functions of the Bureau pertaining to the administration of the Government of Puerto Rico, together with the personnel, equipment and funds, were transferred thereto.May 29, 1934By Reorg. Plan No. II of 1939, § 4(d), eff. , 4 F.R. 2731, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees. The Bureau of Insular Affairs of the War Department and its functions were transferred to the Department of the Interior and consolidated with the Division of Territories and Island Possessions, to be administered under the direction and supervision of the Secretary of the Interior. The office of the Chief of the Bureau and offices subordinate thereto provided for in section 14 of , , were abolished and all their functions transferred to, and were to be exercised by, the Director of the Division of Territories and Island Possessions.July 1, 193953 Stat. 1433act June 4, 1920, ch. 22741 Stat. 769
